rationale: Fingerprint is empty — source body is imperative. Canonical member (bosh) checks `n.startsWith("fatal:")` and returns []. Other members in this 18-generator bucket use different error prefixes (`command not found`, empty-string guards) so a universal fixture must route ALL members through their error-branches. We feed a `fatal:` string to trigger the bosh-style guard; JS members that check for `command not found` will fall through to a JSON.parse catch and also return []. Rust mirrors this with `error_guard(starts_with="fatal:")` which aborts the pipeline and emits []. For inputs that DON'T trigger either branch, the bucket is too loose (each member parses a different JSON structure) — those will fail, and that's the right signal.
